DEFENDANT JAMES SKAGGS' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND/OR DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS DENIED. IT IS UNTIMELY, HAVING BEEN FILED 3 DAYS BEYOND THE STATUTORY DEADLINE (CCP 1008 AND 1013). MOREOVER, DEFENDANT HAS NOT PRESENTED NEW FACTS OR LAW BUT RELIES ON "FACTS MORE FULLY EXPLAINED," AN ARGUMENT THAT IS OUTDATED BY CCP 1008. FINALLY, THE "FACTS MORE FULLY EXPLAINED" DO NOT DEMONSTRATE MISTAKE, SURPRISE, EXCUSABLE NEGLECT OR INADVERTENCE, PARTICULARLY GIVEN THE PRIOR TESTIMONY OF DEFENDANT AND MS. SKAGGS IN OPEN COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, CSR 11452

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default And/Or Defualt Judgemnt
Off Calendar Jul-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JAMES SKAGGS' Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Default And/Or Default Judgment.

Appearances:
J. Michael Phelps (433-3733) for plaintiff and responding party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion is denied. It is untimely, having been filed 3 days beyond the statutory deadline (CCP 1008 and 1013). Moreover, defendant has not presented new facts or law but relies on "facts more fully explained," an argument that is outdated by CCP 1008. Finally, the "facts more fully explained" do not demonstrate mistake, surprise, excusable neglect or inadvertence, particularly given the prior testimony of defendant and Ms. Skaggs in open Court. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/xAM) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452

Default Prove-Up/Quiet Title Hearing.
Off Calendar Apr-03-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: April 3, 2008

This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Judge Ollie Marie Victoire, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Karen Farnsworth, #5162.
J. Michael Phelps, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Vanessa-Rene Montano, who was placed under oath and testified.
D. Alexander Floum, Esq. appeared for defendant James Read Skaggs, who was not present at today's hearing.
There is currently pending a motion to quash service summons set to be heard in Department 302 on April 22, 2008. Mr. Floum's request that today's default prove-up hearing be stayed pending resolution of the afore-mentioned motion to quash service of summons is DENIED.
Default entered against defendant on February 8, 2008.
Doe defendants dismissed today, April 3, 2008.

Plaintiff's Exhibit 1---Grant Deed dated 06/29/94----------------------------------ID/EVID.
Plaintiff's Exhibit 2---Grant Deed dated 03/08/02----------------------------------ID/EVID.
Plaintiff's Exhibit 3---Grant Deed dated 06/20/03----------------------------------ID/EVID.
Plaintiff's Exhibit 4---Grant Deed dated 12/03/04----------------------------------ID/EVID.
Plaintiff's Exhibit 5---Calculation Summary dated 04/03/08-------------------------ID/EVID.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant James Read Skaggs as follows:

Damages/Principal - $16,100.00
Costs - To Be Determined - Subject to submission of a Memorandum of Costs.

Furthermore, the Court specifically finds that defendant James Read Skaggs does not own, and never has owned, any right, title, estate, lien, or interest in 66 Ellsworth Street, San Francisco, CA 94110, and is enjoined for making any further claim to any right to said property.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
